data.title: ESPN BET sportsbooks will be at 19 PENN casinos after the latest expansion
data.description:
PENN Entertainment (NASDAQ:PENN) announced during the week that another 10 retail sportsbooks at key properties will be rebranded to ESPN BET Sportsbook during the early part of 2025.

The list of Hollywood and Ameristar Casino properties that will have sportsbooks rebranded as ESPN BET Sportsbook includes locations in Iowa, Pennsylvania, Ohio, Colorado, Indiana, and West Virginia. PENN's (NASDAQ:PENN) existing ESPN BET retail locations span properties in Kansas, Louisiana, Maryland, Michigan, and Ohio.

Following regulatory approvals, the total number of ESPN BET branded sportsbooks at PENN (PENN) properties will be 19.

Last year, PENN (PENN) released several product enhancements and ESPN integrations to the ESPN BET offering. Those product improvements helped contribute to a higher parlay mix and higher structural hold during Q3. By the end of that quarter, ESPN Bet had close to four million members in its digital database. Looking ahead, PENN (PENN) thinks increased integration across the ESPN ecosystem will help drive more monetization through enhanced engagement, retention, and reactivation.

The ESPN BET app and website launched on November 14, 2023.

  • Filing

data.title: Hershey CEO Michele Buck to retire
data.description:
• The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) announced on Friday that Michele Buck, Chairman of the Board of Directors, President and Chief Executive Officer, has informed the board of her intention to retire from the company effective June 30, 2026.
  • Buck intends to serve in her existing roles until her successor is appointed, at which time she plans to resign from the board and transition to a senior advisor role until her retirement.
  • The board has appointed a special committee to direct the search for the company's next CEO and will consider external and internal candidates, in partnership with a nationally recognized search firm.
  • The Hershey Company (HSY) shares were up 1.04% in post-market trading.

data.title: Blackstone reviewing options for Liftoff business: report
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) is weighing its options for its Liftoff mobile app marketing business, including a sale, Reuters reported Friday, citing two people familiar with the matter.

Reuters quoted the people as saying a sale could value Liftoff at more than $4B, including debt.

The people also reportedly said Blackstone is working with Goldman Sachs and Jefferies about its options for the unit, which may also include an initial public offering.

The review is in an early stage, and a deal is not guaranteed, the people were quoted as saying.

Blackstone and Goldman declined comment when reached by Seeking Alpha. Jefferies did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

data.title: Nokia shares snapped six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Nokia (NYSE:NOK) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock ended 3.1% lower at $4.52 on Friday.

The global technology company added 5.2% in the preceding six sessions. The stock advanced nearly 30% in 2024, dwarfing the 24% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

NOK is up 3% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, NOK has a Hold rating with a score of 3.1 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, B in momentum, while it got a D- growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 6 analysts gave NOK a Buy and above, while five gave it a Hold rating. No analyst has recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Most recently, Nokia (NYSE:NOK) and Openreach introduced intent-based networking to build an open-access network, accelerating UK fiber broadband rollout.

Earlier in December 2024, Nokia was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral by J.P. Morgan due in part to a recovery in telecommunications spending in 2025 and the Finnish company's newly expanded partnership with Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT). "Nokia should also grow in the enterprise market, E.G. the recent expansion of its relationship with Microsoft Cloud."

“Nokia has a strong asset and patent portfolio, but its competitive edge is limited, especially against rivals like Ericsson,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: Estée Lauder ticks higher amid takeover, activist speculation
data.description:
Estée Lauder (NYSE:EL) quickly edged higher by 0.8% amid some takeover and activist speculation around the cosmetics maker.

There appears to be a renewed round of speculation about potential takeover and shareholder activism, according to a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Friday. There's talk that the cosmetics maker may have attracted interest from a possible buyer.

The New York Post reported in May 2023 that the activist investor Nelson Peltz was sniffing around Estée Lauder (EL).

A potential activist push also comes after the WSJ reported in November that board member Jane Lauder, who is a cousin to Chairman William Lauder, wrote a letter to the board in September calling for his ouster. Gordon Haskett's Don Bilson pointed out in November that since Estée Lauder (NYSE:EL) is a family controlled company, so there is only so much that an activist can really do.

Estée Lauder (EL) announced earlier Friday that Ronald S. Lauder will retire from the company's board, effective immediately.

data.title: Sunrun raised at UBS as California market share nearly doubled during past year
data.description:
Sunrun (NASDAQ:RUN) -7.5% in Friday's trading even as UBS upgrades shares of the solar installer to Buy from Neutral with a $17 price target, up from $14, saying the stock is trading near all-time lows despite positive fundamental trends, including significant growth in market share in California and the increasing deployment of battery storage in new projects.

Sunrun's (NASDAQ:RUN) California market share has nearly doubled over the past year to 22%, UBS analyst Jon Windham says, due partly to the company's success in attaching battery storage to ~60% of its new projects, and the deployment of storage solutions surged 126% Y/Y in the three months ending in November 2024, which has expanded the addressable market for the company.

The bank forecasts 15% growth in the U.S. residential solar market in 2025, and the short project cycles for residential solar make the company a key player in providing new capacity to meet the current demands for load growth, the analyst says.

Despite the emerging positive trends, Sunrun (RUN) shares have been trading below the net contracted assets reported in Q3 2024, suggesting the market has not yet priced in future growth, Windham says.

The analyst also notes the fate of renewable tax credits, which could be addressed in the U.S. government's budget reconciliation as early as March, as a potentially key catalyst for Sunrun (RUN) shares.

data.title: Deutsche Bank said to plan 10% boost to investment bank bonuses
data.description:
Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) is planning a roughly 10% increase in bonuses for its investment bankers, aligning their compensation with that of other major Wall Street firms, according to a media report dated Friday.

The planned bonus increase is an average and changes for units will vary, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter, adding that the decision is not yet set in stone and details about individual bonuses will be shared in March.

The move comes as executives at major investment banks have plans to hand out their biggest bonus hikes since the pandemic to traders and dealmakers, the article said, with increases of 10% or more seen for many desks.

Deutsche's (NYSE:DB) investment banking arm grew 12% in the first nine months of 2024, with revenue from deal advice jumping 58% over the same period.

data.title: Verizon says it has contained impact from the Salt Typhoon cyber attack
data.description:
Verizon (NYSE:VZ) provided an update on the Salt Typhoon cyber attack, saying it contained the incident brought on by the threat actor.

The company said Friday, "an independent and highly respected" cybersecurity firm has confirmed the containment by the company.

"In this incident, a small number of individuals primarily involved in government or political activity were targeted by the threat actor. Those customers have been notified of the threat actor’s activity... We have not detected threat actor activity in Verizon’s network for some time, and, after considerable work addressing this incident, we can report that Verizon has contained the activities associated with this particular incident,” the telecom said in a statement.

"During the investigation, Verizon determined that, in the pursuit of a small number of targets, the threat actor also accessed a small percentage of mobile internet access and mobile call records of other Verizon wireless customers. Verizon does not have reason to believe that those customers were targeted or that any banking or financial information or Social Security numbers were exposed," the company added.

On late Sunday, the Wall Street Journal reported that Chinese hackers breached more U.S. telecoms than previously thought during the Salt Typhoon cyberespionage campaign.

Sources told The Journal that Charter Communications (CHTR), Consolidated Communications, and Windstream were compromised. The report said hackers also exploited unpatched network devices from cybersecurity company Fortinet (FTNT) and compromised large network routers from Cisco Systems (CSCO).

T-Mobile U.S. (TMUS) and AT&T (T) were also impacted by the hack, but according to a Bloomberg report earlier this month, AT&T said its networks have been cleared.

Through the Salt Typhoon's campaign, actors linked to China burrowed into America's broadband networks and aimed to establish a foothold within the infrastructure of cable and broadband providers that would allow them to access data stored by telecommunications companies or launch a damaging cyberattack. The Journal broke the story first in September last year.

In October, sources told The New York Times that Chinese hackers targeted data from phones used by incoming president and vice president Donald Trump and JD Vance.

data.title: Hershey to announce CEO Michelle Buck's departure
data.description:
Hershey (NYSE:HSY) will announce that Chief Executive Michelle Buck will depart the snack foods company, Semafor reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

According to the report, Buck will remain in the post and on the board until a successor is found, the people said.

The people reportedly said the handover is aimed at being completed by July 2026.

Buck has been CEO since 2017.

Hershey did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The Hershey Trust, which has nearly 80% of the voting power at the company, last month rejected a takeover offer by Mondelez International (MDLZ). The philanthropic trust also rejected an approach by Mondelez in 2016.
